title: Fix SQL Query to Extract Unique Product Categories

statement.markdown: You are given a SQL query that attempts to extract unique product categories from a database table. However, the query contains a logical error that causes it to return duplicate categories. Your task is to fix the query to return only unique categories, sorted in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: A SQL query string as input
statement.outputFormat: A list of unique product categories, sorted in ascending order, as a comma-separated string
statement.notes: The input query may contain multiple SELECT statements, but only the last one will be executed.

constraints.description: Maximum number of rows in the input table

examples.input: SELECT category FROM products WHERE price > 100 UNION SELECT category FROM products WHERE price < 50
examples.explanation: The input query returns multiple rows, but only the last SELECT statement is executed.

hints.text: Check the query syntax and execution order

editorial.title: Fixing the SQL Query
editorial.intuition: The query should use a DISTINCT keyword to remove duplicates and a single SELECT statement to execute the query.
editorial.bruteForce: Try executing the query as is and analyze the output to identify the duplicates.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a single SELECT statement with a DISTINCT keyword and ORDER BY clause to sort the categories in ascending order.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The query should return only unique categories, sorted in ascending order.
editorial.dryRun: Try executing the fixed query with a sample dataset to verify the output.
commonMistakes: Using UNION instead of a single SELECT statement
Forgetting to use the DISTINCT keyword
